commit.message:
Add CODEOWNERS: @generik0 as global required reviewer (#52)

Creates `.github/CODEOWNERS` to make `@generik0` a required reviewer on
all PRs across the repository.

## Description

- Add `.github/CODEOWNERS` with `* @generik0` — covers every file,
enforcing review approval from the repo owner on all pull requests

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (existing functionality would change)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [x] Build / CI/CD

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Integration tests added / updated
- [x] Manual testing performed

**Test steps:**

1. Open a PR targeting `main` — `@generik0` should appear as a required
reviewer automatically
2. Verify via **Settings → Code and automation → Code owners** that the
file is parsed without errors

## Checklist

- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] My changes follow the project's coding standards
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix or feature works
- [x] New and existing tests pass locally
- [x] No new compiler warnings introduced
- [ ] Documentation updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Dependencies reviewed (if new packages added)

## AI Review Notes

**Focus Areas:**
- Correct CODEOWNERS glob syntax (`*` = all files)
- File placed in `.github/` — one of the three locations GitHub checks
(`.github/`, `docs/`, repo root)

**Context:**
- No prior CODEOWNERS file existed; this is a net-new file

**Known Issues / Intentional Patterns:**
- Single global rule is intentional — no per-directory overrides needed
at this stage

commit.message:
refactor(auth): link-based verification, Telegram OTP login, profile Telegram verify (#98)

## Summary

This PR updates the Flutter mobile app for the auth refactor:

1. **Optional telegram at registration** — field stays in form but is no
longer required.
2. **Link-based registration** — registration submits directly, shows
"check your email" on success (no more code entry screen).
3. **Sign-in OTP icons** — replaced "Login with email code" text button
with two icon buttons: 📧 email OTP + ✈️ Telegram OTP.
4. **Telegram OTP login flow** — new `TelegramOtpRequestSubPage` +
`TelegramOtpVerifySubPage` mirroring the email OTP flow.
5. **Profile Telegram verification** — own profile shows "Verify
Telegram" button (alias set, not verified) or ✅ verified badge.
6. **Resend verification** — simplified `RestoredVerificationSubPage` to
just email + resend link (no code entry).

## New files
- `TelegramOtpLoginRepository` + impl
- `TelegramVerifyRepository` + impl
- `TelegramOtpLoginCubit` + state
- `TelegramVerifyCubit` + state
- `TelegramOtpRequestSubPage` + `TelegramOtpVerifySubPage`

## Modified files
- `profile.dart` + generated: added `isTelegramVerified: bool`
- `register_request.dart` + generated: `telegram` is now `String?`
- `auth_gateway.dart` + impl: 3 new methods
- `paths.dart`: 3 new API paths
- `app_router.dart` + `.gr.dart`: 2 new sub-routes
- `app.dart`: 2 new repository providers
- `auth_page.dart`: `TelegramOtpLoginCubit` added to DI
- `profile_page.dart`: `TelegramVerifyCubit` added via
`MultiBlocProvider`

commit.message:
feat: generator, iterator, proxy conformance fixes (#447)

* fix: generator conformance — @@iterator returns self, return on completed preserves value

- Add Generator.prototype[@@iterator]() that returns the generator itself
  per ECMAScript §27.5.1.2, making generators proper iterables.

- Fix generator_return on completed generators to return {value: VALUE,
  done: true} instead of {value: undefined, done: true}, conforming to
  §27.5.3.4 GeneratorResumeAbrupt step 2a.

- Add tests for @@iterator, non-resumability after return/throw, and
  return-on-completed with/without argument.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix iterator protocol handlers in for-of loop dispatch

- GetIterator: pass 'this' (the iterable) when calling @@iterator method
  so custom iterables that reference 'this' in their Symbol.iterator work
- GetAsyncIterator: same this-binding fix for @@asyncIterator/@@iterator
- IteratorNext: pass 'this' (the iterator) when calling .next() on
  PlainObject iterators so stateful custom iterators work correctly
- CreateArrayFromIterable: add missing String, Generator, and PlainObject
  iterator support so [...generator], [...'hello'], and [...customIterator]
  correctly use the iterator protocol instead of producing empty arrays
- Make dispatch_call_value and dispatch_call_with_this pub(super) so
  dispatch.rs can use them

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(proxy): add missing handler trap extractions and pass target to traps

- build_proxy_handler now accepts target_val and passes it to all
  handler trap closures instead of JsValue::Undefined, conforming to
  ECMAScript 10.5 which requires the target object as the first
  argument to every trap.

- Added extraction of 4 additional handler traps from the JS handler
  object: construct, ownKeys, defineProperty, getOwnPropertyDescriptor.
  Previously only get/set/has/deleteProperty/apply were wired up.

- construct trap converts the returned PlainObject into a JsObject.
- ownKeys trap converts the returned array into Vec<String>.
- getOwnPropertyDescriptor trap parses the returned descriptor object
  into (JsValue, PropertyAttributes).
- defineProperty trap forwards the call to the JS handler function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add execute permission to copilot-guard.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: rewrite booking flow — fix pending slot blocking & payment cleanup

Root cause: when Razorpay payment failed or user closed the dialog,
the 'pending' booking was NEVER cleaned up. It blocked the slot for
15 minutes AND the same user could never retry their own slot.

Backend (booking.go):
- Rewrite CreateBooking: only reject for PAID slots or another user's
  active pending (<5 min). DELETE all non-paid rows before INSERT.
  Same user can always retry freely.
- VerifyPayment: DELETE (not UPDATE→failed) on signature failure
- GetBookedSlots + GetRecommendedSlots: reduce hold from 15→5 min
- Use authenticated user_id from JWT context (more secure)

Backend (scheduler.go):
- Delete pending >10 min and ALL failed (immediate cleanup)

Frontend (BookingCalendar.tsx):
- Add Razorpay modal.ondismiss → cancel pending booking on close
- payment.failed → cancel pending booking immediately
- Razorpay not loaded → cancel pending booking
- Match conflict error strings for slot refresh

Frontend (bookingService.ts):
- Add cancelPendingBooking() → DELETE /bookings/{id}
- Fix verifyPayment indentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(auth): link-based verification, Telegram OTP login, profile Telegram verify (#75)

## Summary

This PR refactors the authentication system:

1. **No Telegram fallback** — removed Telegram bot fallback from email
OTP login.
2. **Link-based registration verification** — replaced 6-digit code with
a clickable link (`GET /auth/verify?token=`).
3. **Telegram OTP as first-class login** — new `POST
/auth/login/telegram/request` + `/verify` endpoints (requires
`is_telegram_verified=True`).
4. **Profile-side Telegram verification** — `POST
/auth/telegram/verify/request` sends email link; `GET
/auth/telegram/verify?token=` sets `is_telegram_verified=True`. Resets
when `telegram_alias` changes.

## New endpoints
- `GET /auth/verify?token=` — link-based email verification
- `POST /auth/login/telegram/request` + `/verify` — Telegram OTP login
- `POST /auth/telegram/verify/request` + `GET /auth/telegram/verify` —
profile Telegram verification

## Model/DB changes
- `Alumni`: `is_telegram_verified` boolean column
- `EmailVerification`: `verification_token` columns; `verification_code`
now nullable
- `TelegramVerifyToken`: new table
- Alembic migration included

## Other
- `telegram_alias` is now optional at registration
- Profile update resets `is_telegram_verified=False` when alias changes
- New email HTML templates

## Env vars needed
- `BACKEND_URL` — public base URL for verification links
- `MINI_APP_URL` — for Open App link in success pages

commit.message:
Align TEMPLATE_AGENTS.md section order with quality standards rules (#53)

The `TEMPLATE_AGENTS.md` had a different section structure and order
than what `knowledge-conventional-contexts-quality.instructions.md`
mandates, causing AI agents to generate AGENTS.md files with wrong
section ordering and anti-pattern content.

## Description

- Rewrote `.ai/templates/TEMPLATE_AGENTS.md` to exactly match the
required section order from the quality standards rules

**Sections removed (explicit anti-patterns per rules):**
- `Overview` — contained business value / problem-solution-impact blocks
(forbidden)
- `Architecture > Components` — file listing table (forbidden)
- `Architecture > Data Flow` — prose list (superseded by sequence
diagrams)
- `Domain Model` — used `classDiagram` + "Business Rules" (rules require
`erDiagram`; business rules are anti-pattern)
- `Requirements` — functional requirements + technical debt checklists
(validation checklists are forbidden)

**Sections added in correct order:**
```
1. TL;DR
2. Non-Negotiables        ← new
3. System Context         ← updated: C4Context + sequenceDiagram + erDiagram guidance
4. Architecture Decisions
5. Key Behaviors          ← new
6. Test References        ← new
7. Quality Constraints    ← new
8. Migration Plans        ← new
9. Changelog
```

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (existing functionality would change)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [x] Documentation update
- [ ] Build / CI/CD

## Testing

- [ ] Unit tests added / updated
- [ ] Integration tests added / updated
- [x] Manual testing performed

**Test steps:**

1. Compare template section order against
`knowledge-conventional-contexts-quality.instructions.md` Required
Structure list — sections and order now match exactly.
2. Verify all anti-pattern content (business value blocks, component
tables, classDiagram, FR/TD checklists) is absent from the template.

## Checklist

- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] My changes follow the project's coding standards
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix or feature works
- [x] New and existing tests pass locally
- [x] No new compiler warnings introduced
- [x] Documentation updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Dependencies reviewed (if new packages added)

## AI Review Notes

**Focus Areas:**
- Confirm every section in the Required Structure list (rules §"Required
Structure", items 1–9) has a corresponding placeholder in the template,
in the same order.
- Confirm no anti-pattern content from rules §"Anti-Patterns (MUST
avoid)" remains in the template.

**Context:**
- The only authoritative source for required sections and order is
`.ai/rules/knowledge-conventional-contexts-quality.instructions.md`.
- Template is used by AI agents to scaffold new AGENTS.md files; wrong
order or anti-pattern boilerplate propagates into every generated file.

**Known Issues / Intentional Patterns:**
- Template retains inline guidance comments (`Omit this section if…`) to
help agents apply the conditional-omit rules correctly at generation
time.

commit.message:
feat(fuzzer): persistent executor, 4 new ops, expanded attack surface

- PersistentQmpExecutor: keeps QEMU alive for --session-length testcases
  (default 500) instead of spawning fresh process every execution.
  Uses reader thread + mpsc channel with recv_timeout for sentinel detection.
  Sentinel = query-version (unique "qemu":{ substring in response).
  Session restarts on crash, timeout, or session-length reached.
  Measured speedup: 11 exec/sec → 38-73 exec/sec (3.5-6.5x improvement).
  ASan workers: 1400ms startup amortised over 50 runs → ~30ms/exec effective.

- New ops 24-27 (total 28 ops, op = b0 % 28):
  24: send-key (QMP) — i8042 PS/2 KBC scancode + SeaBIOS IRQ-1 handler
  25: mouse_move / mouse_button (HMP) — PS/2 AUX port + USB tablet
  26: screendump /dev/null — VGA VRAM readout + display surface blitting
  27: query-pci / query-rx-filter / query-named-block-nodes / rtc-reset

- Expanded constants:
  DEVICE_DRIVERS: 14 → 23 (added usb-storage, usb-audio, i82557a, e1000e,
    vmxnet3, intel-hda, hda-duplex, virtio-scsi-pci)
  QUERY_COMMANDS: 12 → 16 (added query-block, query-netdev, query-migrate,
    query-acpi-ospm-status)
  HMP_COMMANDS: 12 → 15 (added info usb, info balloon, info mice)
  SENDKEY_KEYS: new constant with 30 qcode key names

- ChunkOpMutator: below(24) → below(28)
- 9 new seed files for ops 24-27 including usb-mouse scenario seed
- Seeds array: 35 → 44 entries

- run_fuzzer.sh: ASan workers 4→2, --session-length 500 (vanilla) / 50 (ASan),
  vanilla iterations 200K→500K, ASan timeout 8s→10s

- Removed dead QmpConfigurator / CommandConfigurator (replaced by PersistentQmpExecutor)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
